perturbation.original_text: The term “Evaluation Materials” includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as “confidential”.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Evaluation Materials” includes documentation furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as “confidential”.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'information' is replaced with 'documentation.' The agreement now states Evaluation Material includes Documentation, when it used to include Information. This creates uncertainty, because earlier in the text, information is still being used, in the definition of Evaluation Materials. This can lead to confusion as to what Evaluation Materials actually consist of, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Evaluation Materials” includes documentation furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as “confidential”. The term “Evaluation Materials” does not include information which, you establish through written documentation, (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by you or your representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to you by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by you without violating your obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Evaluation Materials' as including 'information,' while the changed text replaces 'information' with 'documentation' in this definition. The contradicted text uses both 'documentation' and 'information' in defining what is excluded from 'Evaluation Materials'. This change creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'information' as something that can be excluded from 'Evaluation Materials,' but the modified definition of 'Evaluation Materials' no longer explicitly includes 'information'. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict as to whether 'information' can ever be considered 'Evaluation Materials,' leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the explanation and 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not utilize any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'use' is replaced with 'utilize'. Later in the paragraph, the word 'use' is used. It is undefined whether the two mean the same thing. This can lead to confusion as to what is allowed creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will not utilize any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, affiliates, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), and lenders (collectively, “your representatives”) who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree (in writing, if requested by the Company or its advisors), to treat such information confidential in accordance with the agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation type is 'Misaligned Terminology'. The original text uses the word 'use', which is changed to 'utilize'. The Contradicted Text also includes both 'utilize'. The explanation states that it is undefined whether 'use' and 'utilize' mean the same thing, and this can lead to confusion. Although both words are present in the Contradicted Text, the change from 'use' to 'utilize' could potentially lead to disputes about the meaning of both and the obligations of the parties because it can be inferred that they have a difference in meaning. The contradiction is a strong enough that a reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible since the change potentially infers that the two words have distinct meanings.

perturbation.original_text: Non-Disclosure The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated or a disclosure is made prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Disclosure The disclosure of your possible interest in acquiring the Corporation could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated or a disclosure is made prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' is replaced with 'Corporation.' There is now uncertainty on what the Non-Disclosure agreement is referring to. Since it says 'Corporation' and 'Company' it is undefined as to which entity the non disclosure pertains to, creating an in-text contradiction. This can lead to confusion as to which entity the obligations apply to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure The disclosure of your possible interest in acquiring the Corporation could have a material adverse effect on the Company’s business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated or a disclosure is made prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or regulatory authority, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person (including, but not limited to, any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company) the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof, nor will you or any of your representatives make inquiry about the Company’s business to any customer, employee, supplier, creditor or competitor of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Company', while the changed text refers to 'Corporation' and 'Company'. The contradicted text also refers to 'Company'. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the non-disclosure agreement now ambiguously refers to 'Corporation' and 'Company', creating uncertainty as to which entity the non-disclosure pertains, conflicting with the contradicted text, which refers only to 'Company'. This is Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Return of Documents Upon the Company’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Documents Upon the Corporation’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Corporation or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' is replaced with 'Corporation' in the first part of the sentence, creating an in-text contradiction. There is now uncertainty on to whom to return the documents. Since it refers to Corporation and Company, it is undefined as to which entity the obligation to return or destroy applies to. This can lead to confusion as to which entity the obligations apply to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Documents Upon the Corporation’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Corporation or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Corporation' in the phrase 'Upon the Corporation’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Corporation or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.' The contradicted text is the exact same text, which highlights the inconsistency between 'Corporation' and 'Company' regarding to whom the documents must be delivered, and to whom destruction must be certified. This creates ambiguity and directly conflicts with the original intent, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Because the entity in question is undefined, and referring to two entities, the definition applies.
